top_signals.title: Iran Is Tightening Its Grip on the Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Shortly after the outbreak of the war with the United States and Israel, Iran took control of the Strait of Hormuz, a key artery for global oil and gas supplies. By threatening and attacking international shipping, Iran brought maritime traffic to a virtual standstill, giving it significant leverage over its neighbors in the Persian Gulf and the global economy. Now, the Islamic Republic is formalizing its dominance over the strategic chokepoint by imposing a new transit regime. That is despite repeated warnings from the United States, which has…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Inside Ukraine’s Battlefield Innovation Loop
top_signals.summary:
This exclusive Cogs of War interview is with Catarina Buchatskiy, the co-founder and director of analytics at the Snake Island Institute, a Kyiv-based defense analytics center, and Viktoriia Honcharuk, the institute’s director of defense technologies. We asked them to share their views on how Ukraine’s military and defense firms turn battlefield feedback into rapid innovation, what Western investors and defense tech companies can learn from Ukraine, and what a future Ukraine-West defense industrial partnership might look like. Sign Up for Our Newsletter American and allied defense companies often speak about testing their products in Ukraine, but few have consistently done so. Why

The post Inside Ukraine’s Battlefield Innovation Loop appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: USA Rare Earth wins up to $19.3M in DOE backing
top_signals.summary: Federal support strengthens USA Rare Earth’s push to build a Western rare earth supply chain outside China.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Port Houston sees April cargo dip, expects rebound in May
top_signals.summary:
Port Houston reported its first container slowdown since early 2025 in April, though executives say improving May volumes point to a quick recovery.

The post Port Houston sees April cargo dip, expects rebound in May appeared first on FreightWaves.

top_signals.title: Ethereum Layer 2 Zero Network to wind down, joining string of protocols shuttering operations
top_signals.summary: Resources will be redirected to focus on growing Zerion's API and wallet service, the team said in a Thursday statement.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: ICE Brent and ICE WTI Perpetual Futures to Launch on OKX
top_signals.summary: OKX, a blockchain technology and trading company serving more than 120 million customers globally, and Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE), one of the world’s leading providers of financial market technology and data powering global capital markets including the New York Stock Exchange, today announced plans for OKX to launch perpetual futures based on ICE’s Brent Crude [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
